title: GateRunCompleted payload
description: Emitted once per completed corpos-gate run when the run is executed through the measure surface's gate_run action. Carries the aggregated verdict (overall_ok + per-check outcomes) plus the parsed coverage / mutation metrics so the fold can persist an EVENT-SOURCED trend row per project: proj_gate_runs (parent, one row per run) + proj_gate_check_results (child, one row per check). This mirrors the benchmark feature (BenchmarkRunCompleted → proj_benchmark_results) and the study-run feature (StudyRunRecorded → proj_study_runs + proj_study_run_scores): the event is the source of truth and the projection is a denormalised read cache the fold rebuilds from the ledger. The verdict is the SAME core the CLI (`corpos-gate run`) produces — internal/gate.Run over the repo's gate.yml — so the persisted trend is faithful to what a developer or CI sees. Cross-cutting entity (entity_kind='gate_run', envelope project_id null); project rides in the payload for the fold to write the namespaced rows. Coverage/mutation nullable metrics use -1 to signal N/A (the check did not run, was skipped, or produced no parseable metric).

project.description: Project this gate run belongs to. Cross-cutting events carry null project_id in the envelope, so it is mirrored here for the fold to populate proj_gate_runs.project_id / proj_gate_check_results.project_id.

commit_sha.description: The commit SHA the gate ran against, when known (empty for a dirty / uncommitted working-tree run). Stored verbatim so the trend can be joined back to a specific revision.

tier.description: The gate tier run: 'pre-commit', 'pre-push', or 'ci'. Tiers are a superset (ci ⊃ pre-push ⊃ pre-commit); a higher tier runs more checks, so coverage/mutation may be present only at the tier that enables them.

overall_ok.description: True iff every non-skipped check passed — the run's aggregate verdict. Identical to `corpos-gate run --tier=<tier>` exit-0 for the same repo/commit (same internal/gate.Run core).

coverage_pct.description: Aggregate statement-coverage percentage parsed from the coverage check's summary. -1 when the coverage check did not run, was skipped, or produced no parseable total.

branch_pct.description: Branch-coverage percentage. Always -1 today — Go's cover tool reports statement coverage only; the field is reserved for stacks that emit branch coverage.

mutation_score.description: Mutation score (0.0–1.0) parsed from the mutation check's report. -1 when the mutation check did not run, was skipped (go-mutesting absent / disabled), or produced no parseable score. Mutation is report-only and never fails the gate.

duration_ms.description: Total wall-clock time of the gate run in milliseconds — the sum of every executed check's duration.

checks.description: Per-check outcomes in run order. Persisted into proj_gate_check_results as child rows. One entry per check the plan executed (fail-fast may stop early, so a failed run carries fewer entries than the full plan).

name.description: Check name (e.g. 'format', 'vet', 'lint', 'build', 'test', 'coverage', 'vuln', 'mutation', or a custom name).

tier.description: The tier the check is registered at.

ok.description: True if the check passed. A skipped check is also true.

skipped.description: True if the check was skipped (e.g. tool absent, no changed packages).

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock time this check took, in milliseconds.

note.description: Short explanatory text from the check (coverage summary, skip reason, failure snippet). Empty when the check has nothing to add.
